title: Here’s how much a substitute teacher makes in Lexington
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lexington substitute teacher Alice L. earns $10,000-$12,000 annually, working in Lexington, Belmont, and a third district.
Alice's per diem rate ranges from $125 to $150, depending on the school district.
Alice's rent is $3,400 for a two-bedroom in Burlington, covered by her late father's savings and her mother's investments.

title: With playoffs within their grasp, the Bruins are peaking at the perfect time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins secured a fourth consecutive win against the Dallas Stars with a 6-3 victory.
The Bruins have a 98% chance of making the playoffs, following a strong week against top teams.
Boston leads the first wild-card spot in the East by six points after multiple rivals lost games.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A one-family Cape Cod home at 42 Lawsbrook Road in Acton sold for $530,000.
A two-family multi-unit building at 61-63 Foster St. in Arlington sold for $1,260,000.
A condo at 682 Atlantic Ave. in Boston was the highest sale at $14,131,000.

title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye attended the Bruins game at TD Garden, where he surprised a fan in an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' t-shirt.
Maye saluted the fan with a heart sign and a cap tip, showcasing his buzz cut from a charity event.
Earlier, Maye participated in Granite Telecommunications' 'Saving by Shaving' event, aiding Boston Children's Hospital, raising over $4 million.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Doc Rivers, former Celtics coach, will be inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame Class of 2026.
Rivers led the Celtics to an NBA championship in 2008, their first since 1986.
Rivers, currently with the Milwaukee Bucks, has an all-time coaching record of 1,191-861 over 27 seasons.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots quarterback Drake Maye shaved his head at a charity event for Boston Children's Hospital, raising $4.15 million.
Granite Telecommunications donated $2,500 per participant, with CEO Rob Hale matching donations dollar for dollar.
The event included notable figures like former Patriot Joe Andruzzi and Boston Children's CEO Kevin Churchwell.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Officer Sean Cullen faces felony and misdemeanor charges for unsecured storage of a firearm and taser in his vehicle.
Cullen's charges followed a domestic dispute resulting in police requesting his firearms, leading to the discovery of the unsecured weapons.
Cullen, a Boston police officer since 2015, was suspended and released on personal recognizance after pleading not guilty.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: N.H. Governor Kelly Ayotte and a trooper rescued a Massachusetts driver from a fiery crash at Bedford Toll Plaza.
The crash occurred on the Everett Turnpike, and the injured driver was hospitalized with serious injuries.
The toll plaza was closed and traffic redirected off Exit 13 while authorities investigated the incident.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel discussed ongoing trade rumors involving Eagles' A.J. Brown at the NFL owners' meetings in Phoenix.
Vrabel emphasized the importance of Drake Maye's development in leading the offense after New England's Super Bowl appearance.
Patriots are considering bolstering their roster in the 2026 NFL Draft, possibly targeting a tight end or best player available.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, is charged with the murder of his wife in their Belchertown, Massachusetts home.
Opper allegedly told a 911 operator his gun "went off" and he killed his wife, Diane Opper.
Officers found Diane Opper with multiple gunshot wounds; Mark Opper was arrested and held without bail.
